name: S-NCGL (Stochastic Non-Commutative Geometry Logic) Master Equation Engine
description: Implements the full S-NCGL master field equation, including sourced, non-local, and nonlinear terms, as the foundational physics kernel for IRER.
differences: Synthesizes dispersion and beta terms in Fourier space, applies nonlinear saturation and phase shift in real space, and supports both NumPy (debug) and JAX (production) backends.
issues: Early versions suffered from monolithic design, state-bleed, and lack of separation between UI, optimization, and simulation logic.
strengths: Provides the mathematically rigorous foundation for all subsequent variants
Supports both CPU and GPU/TPU execution
compatibility: Core of all IRER physics stacks
mathematics: $\partial_t A = \epsilon A - (1+ib_3)|A|^2A + (1+ic_3)\nabla^2A + S(x) + \Phi(A)$
$\partial_t A = \epsilon A - (1+ib_3)|A|^2A + (1+ic_3)\nabla^2A + S(x) + \Phi(A)$
name: JAX-Agnostic Pivot Engine
description: Engine variant that uses a backend abstraction layer (lazy_load_backend) to switch between NumPy and JAX for logic validation and GPU/TPU acceleration.
differences: Enables unified numerical API, supports JIT compilation and high-performance execution across hardware.
issues: Early versions had concretization and recompilation errors due to non-hashable static constants.
strengths: Portable across CPU, GPU, and TPU
compatibility: Supports both local and cloud/HPC environments
mathematics: S-NCGL, with backend-agnostic implementation.
S-NCGL, with backend-agnostic implementation.

name: Quantule Mapper Protocol Engine
description: Variant with enhanced validation pipeline, implementing the Quantule Mapper Protocol for topological and spectral validation.
differences: Integrates multi-ray sampling, persistence homology, and hard-gate SSE checks.
strengths: Ensures only physically valid, topologically robust results are accepted
compatibility: Required for certified production and scientific publication
mathematics: S-NCGL, with topological and spectral validation.
S-NCGL, with topological and spectral validation.

name: IRER v6 Monolithic Script
description: Early version where the core engine, hunter, scorer, and worker logic are all combined in a single script.
differences: No separation of concerns; UI, optimization, and simulation are tightly coupled.
strengths: Simple to run for initial prototyping
compatibility: Only with early dashboard and local file-based validation
mathematics: S-NCGL, but with inline scoring and no modular feedback.
S-NCGL, but with inline scoring and no modular feedback.

name: Blind 5D Exploration Engine
description: Initial workflow where parameter selection is random or brute-force, with no adaptive feedback.
differences: Lacks historical state awareness, no parent selection or evolutionary optimization.
issues: Cannot focus on promising parameter regions
strengths: Useful for initial parameter space mapping
mathematics: S-NCGL, but parameter sweep is non-adaptive.
S-NCGL, but parameter sweep is non-adaptive.

name: Adaptive Evolutionary Engine (ASTE-Hunter)
description: Implements evolutionary strategies, using elite parent selection and adaptive parameter breeding.
differences: Queries registry for top performers, breeds new parameter sets, targets resonance at k ≈ ln(2).
compatibility: Requires global state registry and parent lineage tracking
mathematics: S-NCGL with evolutionary optimization logic.
S-NCGL with evolutionary optimization logic.
governance_provenance: Partial (parent lineage tracking, no manifest signing)

name: Integrated Simulation Driver
description: Unified orchestration and execution in a single, portable script for reproducibility across local and HPC environments.
differences: Eliminates import errors, ensures identical logic across platforms.
issues: May still be monolithic if not modularized
mathematics: S-NCGL, with deterministic seeding and manifest tracking.
S-NCGL, with deterministic seeding and manifest tracking.

name: S-NCGL v1
description: Original solver using explicit Euler integration.
differences: No adaptive dt, basic boundary conditions.
mathematics: Discretized S-NCGL: $\rho_{t+1} = \rho_t + dt \cdot f(\rho_t, \omega_t)$
Discretized S-NCGL: $\rho_{t+1} = \rho_t + dt \cdot f(\rho_t, \omega_t)$

name: S-NCGL v2 (current)
differences: Vectorized, stability checks, supports MinIO artifact output.
mathematics: See IRER_stack_brief.json, Section: mathematical_formulation
See IRER_stack_brief.json, Section: mathematical_formulation

name: 4D Spatiotemporal Engine
description: Evolved engine supporting 4D stacks (t, x, y, z), streaming time-FFT, and dynamic resonance collapse tracking.
differences: Moves beyond static 3D snapshots, enables temporal evolution analysis.
issues: Requires advanced storage and memory management
compatibility: Requires tiered storage and advanced validation
mathematics: S-NCGL with 4D data management and analysis.
S-NCGL with 4D data management and analysis.

name: Hardware-Integrated Engine
description: Integrates feedback from physical hardware (e.g., LOM, photonic cavities) into the simulation loop.
differences: Calibrates simulation parameters against real-world data.
compatibility: Requires hardware interface and calibration logic
mathematics: S-NCGL, with hardware-calibrated parameters.
S-NCGL, with hardware-calibrated parameters.

name: Modular Microservice-Based Engine (Target/Planned)
description: Fully decoupled, event-driven architecture with separate services for UI, optimization, simulation, validation, and storage.
differences: Each service is independently scalable and replaceable.
mathematics: S-NCGL, modularized and compliant with feedback and provenance protocols.
S-NCGL, modularized and compliant with feedback and provenance protocols.
name: Aletheia-IRER Phase 2 Engine (Planned/Future)
description: Engine variant designed to bridge IRER physics with LLM reasoning, simulating reasoning as informational tension minimization.
differences: Integrates physics-based validation with AI/LLM training pipelines.
issues: Conditional on successful validation of IRER physics
compatibility: Not compatible with current validation-only stack
mathematics: S-NCGL for physics, extended to reasoning dynamics.
S-NCGL for physics, extended to reasoning dynamics.

feedback_loop.name: Certified Logic Microservice Engine (Target/Planned)
feedback_loop.description: Fully decoupled, microservice-based architecture with asynchronous optimization, governance, and validation services.
feedback_loop.differences: Each service is independently scalable and replaceable; implements JSON-RPC and job queues.
strengths: High scalability, maintainability, compliance, and auditability
feedback_loop.mathematics: S-NCGL, modularized and compliant with feedback and provenance protocols.
S-NCGL, modularized and compliant with feedback and provenance protocols.
